text: Overnight FEA: bracket boss area peaks 18°C above ambient under load. Confirms heat-spreader path is the limiter. Two fix options: (A) thicken boss by 1.5mm and copper insert, (B) keep geometry, swap chassis material spec.
(A) adds ~12g per assembly and locks us into a copper insert supplier. (B) might let us re-use a higher-conductivity extrusion we already qualified for the gripper. Worth a side-by-side.
Update on ExtruCo strike: union extending through at least the 22nd. Treating 6+ week resumption as base case. Need a sourcing decision this week or DVT line stops in two weeks.
PR #214 merged. Retest run started. Will share thermal/load curve when complete.

Drop test report for unit 5 attached — 1.2m and 1.5m heights both pass spec, with witness marks at the same bracket region that thermal-failed. Two failure modes converging on the same part. Recommend prioritising the bracket fix.

Standup notes: tracking 4 critical items — bracket thermal, extrusion sourcing, motor driver firmware (closing), and a possible BMS over-temp coupling. Risk score raised on extrusion to High. Carlos and Mei have authority to engage AlumWest for tolerance trial parts.
AlumWest agreed to a trial lot at our tighter tolerance — 2-week lead, premium of 18%. If they hold ±0.10mm, we skip the ECO and ship out of pocket on cost.
Tentative recommendation: option (B) — material spec change. Lower mass impact, leverages an already-qualified supplier on the gripper line. Drafting an ECO.
